# MACRO REPORT — 2026-04-17
**Delivered:** 13:24 UTC | **Generated by:** Claude claude-sonnet-4-6

---

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

The dominant macro narrative today is the Iran-war ceasefire pivot: a 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and Iran's declaration that the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for commercial transit have triggered a dramatic -10 to -14% collapse in crude prices, a surge in gold to near $4,905, and a broad risk-on tone across equities. The single most important variable for today's session is the durability of the ceasefire and progress toward a US-Iran diplomatic settlement — any deterioration would instantly reverse oil's plunge and reignite inflation-stagflation fears. The IMF has already cut its 2026 global GDP growth forecast to 3.1%, warning of near-recession conditions if the conflict worsens [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/imf-cuts-growth-outlook-warns-potential-global-recession-if-iran-war-worsens-2026-04-14/]. The Federal Reserve remains firmly on hold, with multiple officials signalling rates could stay elevated well into 2027 if energy-driven inflation persists [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-15/feds-hammack-rates-likely-hold-good-while-cnbc].

---

## 1. GLOBAL MACRO OVERVIEW

The macro backdrop is defined by two competing forces: a war-driven energy price shock that has pushed US CPI to 3.32% YoY and PPI to +4.0% YoY, and a fragile but powerful diplomatic pivot that is now rapidly deflating those energy prices. March CPI printed at 3.32% YoY — the highest since May 2024 — with monthly consumer prices jumping 0.9%, the biggest gain in nearly four years [Source: https://www.huffpost.com/entry/united-states-wholesale-prices-surged-last-war-iran-energy-prices-soaring_n_69de5fede4b0f26bda6200fd]. Producer prices rose 4.0% YoY in March, with energy surging 8.5% month-over-month [Source: https://www.latimes.com/business/story/2026-04-14/wholesale-prices-rose-4-last-month-as-energy-prices-soared]. These prints firmly anchored the Fed on hold.

The Federal Reserve's posture is hawkish-leaning neutral. Cleveland Fed President Beth Hammack stated rates are "in a good place" and likely on hold "for a good while," explicitly citing the Middle East energy shock layering on top of already-elevated inflation [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-15/feds-hammack-rates-likely-hold-good-while-cnbc]. Chicago Fed President Goolsbee went further, suggesting rate cuts may not arrive until 2027 if oil prices remain elevated [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-14/feds-goolsbee-says-rate-cuts-may-need-wait-until-2027]. New York Fed President Williams confirmed the war is "already driving up inflationary pressures" while noting the Fed is "well positioned to balance risks" [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-16/feds-williams-says-war-already-driving-inflation-pressures]. The effective Fed Funds rate stands at 3.64% as of March [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/FEDFUNDS], with the 10-year Treasury at 4.29% and the 2-year at 3.76%, reflecting a 53bp positive curve slope [Source: https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/DGS10].

Today's session is dominated by the oil price crash. Brent crude fell to $88.84 (-10.61%) and WTI to $81.30 (-14.14%) after Iran's foreign minister announced the Strait of Hormuz would be "completely open" for commercial transit during the ceasefire period [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]. This is a massive disinflationary shock that simultaneously improves the growth outlook and reduces pressure on the Fed. US equity futures rallied sharply on the news, with Dow futures gaining 520 points [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]. The S&P 500 closed Wednesday at 7,041.28 (+0.26%), sitting near all-time highs after recovering all Iran-war losses [Source: Yahoo Finance (^GSPC)].

Corporate earnings are providing additional fundamental support. TSMC reported Q1 net income up 58% and lifted its revenue forecast, citing intact AI chip demand despite geopolitical tensions [Source: https://www.marketscreener.com/news/taiwan-semiconductor-manufacturing-company-limited-reports-earnings-results-for-the-first-quarter-en-ce7e50dddd8fff24]. Bank of America Q1 results showed consumer spending held up despite rising pump prices [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-15-2026]. Netflix results are also in the pipeline. The combination of strong earnings, ceasefire optimism, and the oil price collapse is creating a powerful risk-on cocktail, though the VIX at 17.17 (-4.29%) still signals residual uncertainty about ceasefire durability [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

The ECB faces an unusually complex decision with its upcoming meeting. Markets price at least 50bp of ECB hikes by year-end, reflecting sticky European inflation amplified by energy costs, but ECB officials are divided on whether to hike or hold [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html]. The Bank of Japan's Ueda is signalling a cautious stance, managing expectations around rate hikes as imported energy costs weigh on Japan's inflation dynamics, with Standard Chartered calling for two BoJ hikes in H2 2026 [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/video/2026/04/17/bojs-ueda-signals-central-banks-cautious-stance-standard-chartered.html].

## 2. CONFLICT / GEOPOLITICAL STATUS

- Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" for all commercial vessels during the ceasefire period, triggering a ~10% single-session collapse in crude oil prices. [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]
- President Trump announced a 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire commencing Thursday at 5 p.m. ET; US stock futures rallied sharply on the announcement with Dow futures +520 points (+1.1%). [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]
- The US military expanded its blockade of Iran to "all ships, regardless of nationality," pursuing them beyond the Middle East, per Gen. Dan Caine, Chairman of the Joint Chiefs. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-16-2026]
- A key Pakistani mediator was reported in Tehran and the Trump administration talked up prospects for a deal to open the Strait of Hormuz, though no date or venue for a second round of talks has been secured. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-16/gold-rises-middle-east-optimism-calms-inflation-fears]
- The IMF warned the global economy could "teeter on the brink of recession" if the conflict worsens and oil stays above $100/barrel through 2027, having already cut 2026 global GDP growth to 3.1%. [Source: https://www.reuters.com/business/imf-cuts-growth-outlook-warns-potential-global-recession-if-iran-war-worsens-2026-04-14/]
- Commodity currency analysts at Amundi and Ninety One note that regardless of war outcome, Norway, Canada, Australia, and New Zealand FX are well placed to outperform given a structural commodity re-ordering of global supply chains. [Source: https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-17/commodities-reshape-geopolitics-currency-pecking-order-gets-reset]

## 3. FOREIGN EXCHANGE

### Major Pairs

**EUR/USD — ~1.1843** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- The euro is benefiting from broad USD weakness as the DXY slides to 97.683 (-0.55%), with the oil price collapse reducing energy-import cost pressure on Europe and improving the eurozone's terms of trade. However, ECB rate uncertainty caps upside. [Source: Yahoo Finance (EURUSD=X)]
- Key levels: 1.1750 support | 1.1950 resistance
- Catalyst: ECB rate decision (upcoming); ceasefire durability and US-Iran diplomatic talks progress

**GBP/USD — ~1.3582** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- Sterling is holding near multi-month highs versus the dollar, with the pair up a modest 0.06% on the day as the oil shock disinflationary impulse is broadly dollar-negative. UK energy import cost relief from the Hormuz opening is a mild GBP positive. [Source: Yahoo Finance (GBPUSD=X)]
- Key levels: 1.3450 support | 1.3700 resistance
- Catalyst: UK CPI data; BoE communications; ceasefire extension news

**USD/JPY — ~158.354** | Bias: Neutral
- Dollar-yen is marginally lower (-0.29%) as yen finds modest safe-haven support unwind on ceasefire news, but BoJ Governor Ueda's cautious stance on rate hikes keeps the yen structurally pressured. Standard Chartered sees two BoJ hikes in H2 2026. [Source: Yahoo Finance (USDJPY=X)]
- Key levels: 156.50 support | 160.00 resistance
- Catalyst: BoJ rate decision/guidance; Japan CPI; ceasefire durability

**AUD/USD — ~0.7212** | Bias: Bullish
- AUD is the standout FX outperformer (+0.51%), consistent with analyst views that commodity currencies are well positioned in the current geopolitical regime. Copper's modest gains (+0.49%) and China's relative resilience support the Aussie. [Source: Yahoo Finance (AUDUSD=X)]
- Key levels: 0.7100 support | 0.7350 resistance
- Catalyst: China macro data; RBA communications; iron ore and copper price direction

**USD/CAD — ~1.3661** | Bias: Neutral-Bear
- The Canadian dollar is strengthening (USD/CAD -0.51%) as oil-price volatility swings — notably, today's massive crude decline is a negative CAD impulse, but the ceasefire peace-dividend and broader USD weakness are providing partial offset. Net effect is modest CAD gain. [Source: Yahoo Finance (USDCAD=X)]
- Key levels: 1.3550 support | 1.3800 resistance
- Catalyst: WTI crude direction; Canadian CPI; BoC rate decision

**USD/CHF — ~0.7813** | Bias: Bearish
- USD/CHF remains near structurally depressed levels as the dollar weakens and the franc retains safe-haven premium. The oil collapse reduces global recession risk modestly but geopolitical uncertainty keeps CHF bids intact. [Source: Yahoo Finance (USDCHF=X)]
- Key levels: 0.7700 support | 0.7950 resistance
- Catalyst: SNB communications; Swiss CPI; geopolitical de-escalation/escalation

**NZD/USD — ~0.5922** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- The kiwi is modestly higher (+0.17%), tracking the commodity currency complex and benefiting from USD softness. Amundi's call for commodity currency outperformance applies here, though NZD's smaller commodity basket limits the premium. [Source: Yahoo Finance (NZDUSD=X)]
- Key levels: 0.5820 support | 0.6050 resistance
- Catalyst: RBNZ communications; China trade data; global risk sentiment

**EUR/JPY — ~187.492** | Bias: Neutral
- Cross is essentially flat (-0.01%) as ECB rate hike expectations and BoJ caution roughly offset each other. The pair remains elevated and is sensitive to any ECB hawkish surprise or BoJ pivot signals. [Source: Yahoo Finance (EURJPY=X)]
- Key levels: 184.50 support | 190.00 resistance
- Catalyst: ECB decision; BoJ Ueda commentary; eurozone inflation data

**GBP/JPY — ~215.10** | Bias: Neutral
- GBP/JPY is slightly lower (-0.21%) as yen finds modest support from reduced geopolitical risk premia on ceasefire news. The cross remains historically elevated and vulnerable to any BoJ surprise. [Source: Yahoo Finance (GBPJPY=X)]
- Key levels: 212.00 support | 218.00 resistance
- Catalyst: BoJ policy; UK labour/CPI data; Middle East peace deal progress

**EUR/GBP — ~0.8716** | Bias: Neutral-Bull
- EUR/GBP is edging higher (+0.20%) as ECB rate hike expectations provide marginal euro support versus sterling. The pair is range-bound and near-term direction depends on relative central bank signalling. [Source: Yahoo Finance (EURGBP=X)]
- Key levels: 0.8650 support | 0.8800 resistance
- Catalyst: ECB vs BoE policy divergence; eurozone vs UK growth differentials

## 5. GLOBAL EQUITY INDICES

### US Equities

The S&P 500 is at 7,041.28 (+0.26%) as of April 16 close, having recently notched new intraday all-time highs above 7,008 [Source: https://www.barrons.com/livecoverage/stock-market-news-today-041526]. The index has now fully recovered its Iran-war losses and is approximately 2% above pre-conflict levels. Today's Hormuz opening announcement is expected to extend the rally. The Nasdaq 100 is at 26,333.00 (+0.49%), posting an 11-day win streak led by tech, with TSMC's blowout Q1 results (net income +58%) underpinning AI semiconductor confidence [Source: https://www.marketscreener.com/news/taiwan-semiconductor-manufacturing-company-limited-reports-earnings-results-for-the-first-quarter-en-ce7e50dddd8fff24]. The Dow Jones stands at 48,578.72 (+0.24%), with Bank of America's Q1 results showing consumer resilience despite pump price pressures [Source: https://www.wsj.com/livecoverage/stock-market-today-dow-sp-500-nasdaq-04-15-2026]. Netflix results are a key near-term earnings catalyst for the Nasdaq. VIX has declined to 17.17 (-4.29%), signalling reduced but not eliminated volatility risk [Source: Yahoo Finance (^VIX)].

## 8. WEEK AHEAD / SCENARIO ANALYSIS

| SCENARIO | PROBABILITY | TRIGGER | BRENT | S&P 500 | BTC |
|----------|-------------|---------|-------|---------|-----|
| Bull | 35% | Ceasefire extends; formal US-Iran talks announced; oil stays below $90; Fed signals rate cut path for H2 2026 | $82 | 7,250 | $84,000 |
| Base | 45% | Ceasefire holds for 10 days but no formal peace deal; oil stabilises $85–$95; Fed holds; earnings season beats | $90 | 7,050 | $77,000 |
| Bear | 20% | Ceasefire collapses; Strait of Hormuz re-closes; oil spikes back above $100; inflation expectations re-anchor higher | $108 | 6,600 | $68,000 |

### Key Upcoming Catalysts — Week of April 17-25, 2026

1. **IMF-World Bank Spring Meetings (Week of April 21)** — IMF managing director Georgieva expected to deliver updated growth scenarios tied to ceasefire outcomes; any downgrade to the adverse scenario will pressure risk assets. [Source: https://www.latimes.com/business/story/2026-04-14/iran-war-will-slow-global-growth-even-if-ceasefire-holds-imf-warns]

2. **ECB Rate Decision (Upcoming, ~2 weeks)** — Markets price 50bp+ of ECB hikes by year-end; the decision is live and could trigger major EUR/USD and European equity repricing. [Source: https://www.cnbc.com/2026/04/16/ecb-interest-rates-hike-inflation-iran-washington.html]

3. **Netflix Q1 Earnings (This Week)** — A key barometer for Nasdaq 100 momentum; consensus expects resilient subscriber growth despite consumer confidence headwinds. [Source: https://www.marketwatch.com/livecoverage/s-p-500-dow-jones-nasdaq-iran-war-cease-fire-extension-netflix-tsmc-results]

4. **Ceasefire Expiry / US-Iran Diplomacy Update (Within 10 Days)** — The 10-day Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and Hormuz transit declaration will expire; any progress toward or failure of a formal US-Iran peace deal is the highest-impact binary event for oil, FX, and global equities. [Source: https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/17/investing/oil-strait-hormuz-iran]

5. **EIA Weekly Crude Storage Report (Thursday, April 23)** — First post-Hormuz-opening storage print; a large inventory build would confirm the disinflationary oil impulse is sustained, while a draw would suggest supply constraints remain. [Source: https://www.wsj.com/finance/commodities-futures/u-s-natural-gas-futures-gain-ahead-of-storage-data-219dffd7]

### Disputed

- [Report states Brent crude "fell 9.5%, to just below $90 per barrel" and WTI "sank 9.6% to $82.60 per barrel" per the CNN ceasefire article, yet the data bundle shows Brent at -10.61% ($88.84) and WTI at -14.14% ($81.30)]: The CNN article cited intraday figures that differ materially from the bundle's closing/current values — the report uses both sets of numbers without flagging the discrepancy. WTI's -14.14% move per the bundle is significantly larger than the -9.6% cited in the CNN story, and the report uses the CNN narrative framing of "~10% decline" in the executive summary while the bundle reflects a larger WTI move.
- [Report attributes the Pakistani mediator detail to the Kitco/Reuters gold article]: The Kitco gold article (https://www.kitco.com/news/off-the-wire/2026-04-16/gold-rises-middle-east-optimism-calms-inflation-fears) does mention a "key Pakistani mediator in Tehran," which is confirmed. However, the WSJ article (04-16 livecoverage) provides broader context including Trump stating the war is "very close to over" — the report does not include this quote, slightly underweighting the bullish diplomatic signal.
- [Report characterises gold's move as a "record high" at $4,904.60]: The data bundle confirms the current gold price but no historical price series is provided to independently verify this constitutes an all-time record. The Kitco gold article references a "one-month high" as of April 16 at $4,819.66, and the Bitget article references gold "near $4,840." While gold at $4,904.60 exceeds those levels, the "record high" claim cannot be fully verified from the bundle alone.
- [Report cites Bitmine's quarterly net loss as "$3.8B" in the body text but "$3.6B" in the crypto section header citing CoinDesk]: The CoinDesk article title states "$3.6 billion loss" while the article body states "$3.8 billion quarterly net loss." The report uses $3.8B in the body (consistent with article text) but the section header referencing the CoinDesk title appears in the raw data as "$3.6 billion" — minor inconsistency in the source itself, but the report correctly uses $3.8B.
- [Report attributes the Solana DoubleZero Edge and Tether/Drift details to the Decrypt URL about the Foundation NFT platform shutdown]: The Decrypt URL is a news explorer page that aggregates multiple stories; DoubleZero and Tether/Drift headlines appear as sidebar items on that page rather than as the primary article. This is a weak/indirect source attribution, though the information does appear in the bundle.

---

### Additional Insights

1. **WTI/Brent spread widening is an actionable signal**: WTI (-14.14%) is falling nearly 3.5 percentage points more than Brent (-10.61%), producing an unusually wide WTI discount. This spread compression is consistent with the US blockade redirecting Iranian oil supply dynamics — US domestic crude faces different demand/supply pressures than global Brent. Traders long energy spreads (long Brent/short WTI) may find this dislocation worth monitoring; a ceasefire collapse would likely narrow the spread sharply as WTI bounces harder on a Hormuz re-closure.

2. **DXY at 97.683 is a structurally important level**: The dollar index has fallen -0.55% to 97.683 — a level not seen since before the Iran war began. If the ceasefire holds and oil deflation becomes entrenched, the disinflation narrative removes one of the Fed's key reasons to stay hawkish, and the DXY could accelerate lower. This has direct implications for AUD/USD (highest-ranked FX trade in the report) and gold, both of which have strong inverse DXY correlations. Commodity currency longs and gold longs benefit doubly from a sustained DXY breakdown.

3. **Gold-to-oil ratio has surged to historically extreme levels**: Gold is at $4,904.60 while Brent is at $88.84, giving a gold/oil ratio of approximately 55x. Under normal geopolitical conditions, this ratio runs 20–30x. Even accounting for war premium in gold and war premium in oil collapsing simultaneously, the current ratio suggests either gold is significantly overvalued relative to oil or oil has further downside. Traders should monitor whether gold gives back gains as the inflationary oil shock fades — a ceasefire-driven gold correction is a real risk if the Fed's rate-hold rationale softens materially.

4. **The 10Y-2Y curve slope of +53bp is a quiet risk signal**: The curve has re-steepened to 53bp (10Y: 4.29%, 2Y: 3.76%), which in the current context reflects stagflation uncertainty — long-end inflation risk premia elevated while short-end reprices Fed cuts further out. If the Hormuz opening persists and CPI rolls over in April data (to be released in coming weeks), the 2Y could rally (yield drops) faster than the 10Y, causing curve re-inversion. Rate-sensitive financials and housing would respond strongly to a 2Y yield move. Watch for 3.50% on the 2Y as a key level.

5. **KOSPI at 6,191.92 is anomalously high relative to global peers and warrants scrutiny**: The KOSPI is down only -0.55% today despite Nikkei falling -1.75% and ASX flat. The bundle shows KOSPI at 6,191.92 — for context, Korea's heavy semiconductor exposure should logically be a tailwind given TSMC's blowout results and AI demand confirmation. However, the index's level (6,191 on the KS11) appears unusually elevated versus historical norms. Traders should verify data integrity here, as an index value of ~6,191 for KOSPI is significantly above its typical trading range (historically ~2,000–2,800
